title: cyperus ohwii
intro: Cyperus ohwii, also known as Cyperus esculentus var. ohwii, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the Cyperaceae family, often found in wetland areas and characterized by its yellow flowers and edible rhizomes. The rhizomes of the Cyperus ohwii plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cyperus ohwii include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Cyperus ohwii is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used in cooking, particularly in traditional Asian cuisines.
